key_design_decisions: Metric set: 6 per-run integers + 3 derived (new/closed/net velocity) + JSONL append. Scope is narrow (per directive's 'not the kitchen sink' constraint).

Calibrated thresholds against 36-day reconstructed historical curve (Apr 6 → May 11). Operator escalation tier set to fire 1-13 days before operator-noticed-by-accident. Leading indicators (status_count + velocity + age14d) fire 6-18 days early.
Backfill strategy: bootstrap queue-history.jsonl with git-log-reconstructed historical rows so trend metrics work from day 1 — no 7-14 day blind period.
Ownership: queue-history.jsonl is workspace-owned (tinker writes from FUSE each run), workspace-sync pushes hourly. NOT in NEVER_PUSH.
Explicit NOT-in-scope carve-out: no auto-burndown, no auto-close, no Step 1-3 rewrite, no other-agent behavior changes.

calibration_validation_against_today.method: Reconstructed open-issues.json counts from git log of monitor/decisions/open-issues.json over 36 days. Latest commit per day.
calibration_validation_against_today.interpretation: Calibration is in the design sweet spot. Operator would have seen escalating findings 13+ days before manual discovery.
